title: Fishing the Falls
desc: Glowfin eels live in the mist. Catching them is the easy part.

steps.text:
Glowfin eels are a delicacy in Kiru City — translucent, bioluminescent, and incredibly fast. They live in the mist zone at the base of Cordac Falls, where the water is more air than liquid. Catching them requires a special net made of spider-silk thread and absolutely no dignity.

A fisher is waist-deep in the spray, laughing at nothing. She spots you on the bank. "Want to try? The eels are running. You just have to wade in and wave the net around until something glows."

The mist is freezing. The rocks are slippery. The eels, presumably, are fast.

options.label: Watch from the bank and learn first

steps.text:
The water hits you like ice. Within seconds you're soaked and shivering, visibility reduced to arm's length by the spray. You wave the net in slow arcs, feeling foolish.

Then a streak of blue-green light rockets past your knee. An eel. You swing — miss. Another streak, this one golden. Swing — the net snags something. You haul it up. A glowfin eel, the length of your forearm, pulsing with soft amber light.

It's beautiful. It's also vibrating like a tuning fork and trying very hard to escape.

You catch three more before your hands go numb. The fisher is impressed. "Four on your first outing. Natural." She helps you to the bank and wraps the eels in wet cloth. They glow through the wrapping like little lanterns.

"You keep two. I keep two. Fair?"

You watch the fisher work. She doesn't use sight — in the mist, you can't. She uses the glow. When an eel passes, it leaves a light-trail in the spray, and she sweeps the net through the trail a half-second behind where the eel was.

"They're fast, but predictable," she explains. "They follow the current eddies. Read the water, read the eel."

You try her technique. Read the water. Watch for the glow. Sweep behind the trail. On your third attempt, you feel the net go heavy. An eel, jade-green and furious, thrashing against the silk.

You catch two more using the trail method. The fisher nods approvingly. "Most people never figure that out. They just flail. You listened."

She gives you the three eels and the net. "Keep both. I've got a dozen already."

options.label: Head home with glowing eels and a new skill
